sn.s: Mir händ Plän gha, am Samschtig an See z'gah.
sn.e: We had plans to go to the lake on Saturday.

sn.s: Aber em Lukas sis Auto isch kapütt gange.
"De Plan isch hi," seit er, truurig.
"Ruhig, mir sueched en Plan B," antworte ich.
sn.e: "Easy, we'll find a plan B," I answer.
"What do we do then?" he asks.

sn.s: "Mir chönd de Zug näh. Mir werded chli spöter cho."
sn.e: "We can take the train. We'll arrive a bit later."

sn.s: "Aber mir händ trotzdäm de ganz Namittag," füeg ich dezue.
sn.e: "But we'll still have the whole afternoon," I add.

sn.s: "Gueti Idee. Daa han ich nöd dänkt."
sn.e: "Good idea. I hadn't thought of that."

sn.s: "Morn reparirsch s'Auto und alls isch guet."
sn.e: "Tomorrow you fix the car and all's well."

sn.s: Wänn en Plan nöd klappt, suecht me en andere.
sn.e: When a plan doesn't work, you find another.

sn.s: Über d'Zuekunft rede: mir werded, mir gönd, mir chömed.
sn.e: Talking about the future: we will, we'll go, we'll come.
Being flexible is part of life.

sn.s: Am Schluss zellt nu, dass mir zäme sind.
sn.e: In the end all that counts is being together.
